pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to the ciklum-search-engine project: what it does, its technology stack, and how the modules fit together. Links to child pages for architecture details and getting started.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Step-by-step guide for setting up the development environment: prerequisites (Java 23, Maven, MongoDB), building the project with Maven Wrapper, running locally, and Docker Compose setup for MongoDB.

pages.title: Project Structure and Build System
pages.purpose: Explains the multi-module Maven layout (api-rest, application, domain, infrastructure, boot), the root POM, shared dependency management, and how Lombok and MapStruct annotation processors are configured across all modules.

pages.title: Architecture
pages.purpose: Overview of the hexagonal / clean architecture pattern used in the project, showing how the five modules (domain, application, api-rest, infrastructure, boot) relate to each other and the direction of dependencies. Links to child pages for each layer.

pages.title: Domain Layer
pages.purpose: Detailed coverage of the domain module: the Project entity, the BaseProjectQuerySearch filter with its builder, QueryPagination cursor-based pagination, the SearchProjectsUseCase interface, and the ProjectSearchRepository port.

pages.title: Application Layer
pages.purpose: Covers the application module: SearchProjectUseCaseImpl orchestration logic, the QueryFormatter chain-of-responsibility interface, and DefaultQueryFormatter. Explains how the use case formats queries and delegates to the repository port.

pages.title: Infrastructure Layer
pages.purpose: Covers the infrastructure module: MongoDB persistence via ProjectSearchCompoundRepositoryImpl and GenericAggregationRepository, the CompoundSearchService that builds Atlas Search compound queries, the builder types (SearchCompoundCriteria, SortCriteria, Operator enum), the BaseProject MongoDB document entity, and the MapStruct ProjectMapper.

pages.title: REST API Layer
pages.purpose: Overview of the api-rest module: how HTTP requests enter the system, the controller-service-validator pipeline, and the DTO contract. Links to child pages for the controller, DTOs, and validation.

pages.title: Controllers and Request Pipeline
pages.purpose: Detailed explanation of SearchProjectsController (POST /v1/projects), AbstractSearchController generic base class (template method pattern), and how the validate → buildQuerySearch → use-case execution pipeline works.

pages.title: Service Orchestration and DTOs
pages.purpose: Covers SearchProjectService: how it validates the request, maps ProjectSearchRequestDTO to BaseProjectQuerySearch, invokes SearchProjectsUseCase, and transforms the List<Project> result into ProjectSearchResponseWrapperDTO. Also documents all DTO classes and the ResponseRestMapper.

pages.title: Validation
pages.purpose: Explains the SearchRequestValidator<T> interface (extending Spring Validator), how validators are injected into AbstractSearchController as a list, and the pluggable strategy pattern that allows adding new validation rules without modifying controller logic.

pages.title: Data Storage and MongoDB Integration
pages.purpose: Overview of how the service connects to MongoDB (Atlas and local Docker), the Search_engine database, and the Atlas Search index. Links to child pages for configuration and the search index setup.

pages.title: Configuration and Connection
pages.purpose: Documents the application.yml MongoDB URI (Atlas SRV connection string), the Search_engine database, local Docker Compose MongoDB setup, replica set configuration for local development, and security considerations around hardcoded credentials.

pages.title: Atlas Search Index and Seed Data
pages.purpose: Explains the dyn_idx0 Atlas Search index definition (autocomplete on name, token/string on tags), the JavaScript seed scripts that create the index and insert sample project documents, and how the infrastructure layer uses the index for compound search queries.

pages.title: Glossary
pages.purpose: Definitions of all codebase-specific terms, abbreviations, design patterns, and domain concepts used throughout the search-engine project. Each entry includes file/class pointers for quick navigation.
